title: The Swallow and the Crow
lines: A Swallow and a Crow were disputing about the color of their feathers.
The Crow said, 'My color is more noble than thine; I am dark, the color of warriors and kings.'
The Swallow answered, 'My colors are more beautiful; I am striped with white and blue and red.'
They could not agree, so they went to a judge to decide the matter.
'Each of you," said the judge, 'is beautiful in his own way.'
'What is most important is not the color of your feathers, but the goodness of your heart.'
moral: Beauty is in the eye of the beholder.
